assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies this as a webhook compatibility issue after Kubernetes upgrade and explains that webhooks are preventing pod creation

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Response provides kubectl get validatingadmissionwebhooks and kubectl get mutatingadmissionwebhooks commands to identify problematic webhooks

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Response provides Option 1 to delete webhooks and Option 2 to add failurePolicy: Ignore as immediate fixes

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Response includes specific kubectl delete commands for both validating and mutating admission webhooks

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: While the response mentions these are temporary fixes, it doesn't explicitly warn that deleting webhooks removes validation capabilities

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Response includes checking cert-manager version and references compatibility matrix for k8s 1.32

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Response advises upgrading cert-manager to v1.15+ for k8s 1.32 compatibility as the proper fix

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Response provides failurePolicy: Ignore as Option 2, presenting it as an alternative to deletion

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Response includes kubectl run test-pod command with --dry-run=server to test pod creation

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the need to persist changes in source of truth systems like Helm charts or GitOps manifests

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: While the response mentions it as temporary, it doesn't explicitly state the risks of using failurePolicy: Ignore or recommend reverting to Fail after resolution
